vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially accurate but incomplete. P3 (Pothos/Rabbit) is present. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are represented by a single, struggling specimen in the black pot. No other plants are visible.

inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (No evidence of Mexican Mint)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Present (Black Pot, likely the single stem identified)
New_Introductions: Eggshell fragments used as soil top-dressing/calcium supplement

plant_audit.P3: Healthy, single leaf visible; rabbit anchor remains in situ.
plant_audit.P4: Single, elongated stem with one leaf; appears to be the sole survivor in the black pot.

biome_observations.debris: Significant presence of crushed eggshells on the soil surface of the black pot (P4).

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a transition from a more populated state to a simplified, singular-stem state in the black pot. The eggshell fragments were introduced between T-5 and T-4 and have remained static since.

visual_health_inference.P4: High stress; the single leaf is thin and the stem is fragile, suggesting poor light or nutrient uptake issues.

anomalies: Eggshell fragments in P4 soil (intentional intervention).
Absence of P2 (Mexican Mint) suggests either total senescence or misidentification in the registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of minimal maintenance. The most significant event in the observed window was the introduction of eggshell fragments to the black pot, likely an attempt to amend soil pH or calcium levels for the remaining P4 specimen. The P3 Pothos remains the most robust element of the collection, while the P4 specimen shows signs of etiolation or struggle. The yellow pot (P1) remains largely unchanged throughout the observation period.
